summary: Tested all new features: Templates API, Calendar Sync, 90-min buffer, PDF contract. Found and fixed 3 critical bugs. All 22 backend tests pass. All frontend tests pass. Admin login, Templates page (12 event types, stats, expand/collapse, save, search, variable pills), Calendar connect/disconnect all working correctly.

critical.issue: FIXED: Status stored as 'accept'/'reject' instead of 'accepted'/'rejected'. This broke contract download (400 for accepted invites), 90-min buffer checks (queries status=='accepted'), and Calendar invite filtering. Root cause: line 282 `update = {"status": data.action, ...}` - action verbs were not mapped to canonical status strings.

FIXED: Pydantic model TemplateUpsert had required field `event_type: str` but frontend only sends form fields (platform_message, whatsapp_template_name, etc.) without event_type in body - caused 422 Unprocessable Entity on every template save from frontend.
critical.fix_applied: Changed `event_type: str` to `event_type: Optional[str] = None` in templates.py since event_type is already in URL path

ui_bugs.issue: FIXED: Error toast tried to render Pydantic array `[{type, loc, msg, input, url}]` as React child causing 'Objects are not valid as a React child' crash. Occurred when 422 from backend returned array-type detail.
ui_bugs.fix_applied: Added typeof check: `const msg = typeof detail === 'string' ? detail : 'Failed to save template'`

issues: Stats bar shows 0/0/0 briefly during loading before data arrives (minor flicker - acceptable UX)

critical_code_review_comments: respond_to_invite in gigs.py had the action→status bug fixed but also consider: lead_accept_counter correctly uses 'accepted' - keep consistency
TemplateUpsert model now has Optional event_type which is unused body param - clean approach since URL path is source of truth
Email mocking (Resend) logs to DB correctly when no API key - working as expected (MOCKED)
Google Calendar (MOCKED) properly updates DB and returns correct connect/disconnect status
PDF generation with reportlab is REAL - verified generates valid PDF bytes with %PDF magic header
seed_data_creation: Created test users via /api/auth/register, test gigs, and test invites (accepted) during backend tests. Template saved for invite_sent event type.
context_for_next_testing_agent: 3 bugs found and fixed. Backend 22/22 pass. Frontend fully functional. Key areas: (1) Admin /admin/templates page works - 12 templates, expand/collapse, save, search, variable pills all working. (2) Calendar connect/disconnect toggle works with mocked API. (3) PDF contract generation works (real reportlab). (4) 90-min buffer correctly returns 409. Admin: admin@crewbook.in / Admin@123. MOCKED: WhatsApp, Email (Resend), Google Calendar OAuth.

rca_of_issues.critical_bug: In routers/gigs.py respond_to_invite(), the invite status was set from data.action directly (e.g., 'accept') instead of the canonical status string ('accepted'). Only the 'counter' action was correctly overridden to 'counter_offered'. Fix: Added _STATUS_MAP = {'accept': 'accepted', 'reject': 'rejected', 'counter': 'counter_offered'} and applied mapping. Reproduction: Accept an invite then try to download contract -> gets 400 instead of PDF.
rca_of_issues.templates_422: TemplateUpsert Pydantic model had `event_type: str` as required field. Frontend's handleSave only sends form fields (no event_type). Backend returned 422, which had array-format detail that React couldn't render as toast text, causing runtime crash. Fix: Made event_type Optional in model; fixed frontend error handler to check typeof detail.
